commitResults.messageBody: Fix use cases mentioned UI elements and add preconditions
Fix Email.java equals method and hashcode

fix: resolve delete student against filtered list (#246)

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: resolve delete student against filtered list (#246)
commitResults.messageBody:
After running find inside a selected tutorial, DeleteCommand was still
indexing into currentTutorial.getStudents() (the unfiltered list), which
did not match the indices the user saw in the UI. This could silently
delete the wrong student.

Resolve the target from model.getFilteredStudentList() instead, so the
displayed index maps to the displayed student. Still verify membership
in the current tutorial before removing.

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: reject tutorials that overlap an existing tutorial (#271, #248)
commitResults.messageBody:
The app models a single tutor's schedule, so two tutorials can never
run at the same time. There was no such check: AddCommand accepted any
new tutorial as long as its code was unique, which both let a tutor
double-book themselves (#248) and made it possible for the same
student to end up in overlapping tutorials (#271).

Add a pair of pure helpers on the value objects:
- TimeSlot.overlapsWith compares parsed start/end times; touching
  boundaries (10:00-11:00 vs 11:00-12:00) are NOT treated as overlap.
- Tutorial.overlapsWith returns true only when the day matches AND
  time slots overlap; a tutorial never overlaps with itself.

Wire this into AddCommand's tutorial branch: before accepting a new
tutorial, scan the full tutorial list and reject with a message that
names the conflicting tutorial code. Cover the two typical cases in
AddCommandIntegrationTest (strict overlap rejected, back-to-back slot
allowed).

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: require selected tutorial for edit command (#256)
commitResults.messageBody:
AddCommand and DeleteCommand both refuse to operate on a student when
no tutorial is selected, but EditCommand silently ran against the
global student list. This was inconsistent and let users mutate
records they could not see in the current view.

Add the same MESSAGE_NO_CURRENT_OPERATING_TUTORIAL guard to
EditCommand so all three student-mutating commands behave the same.

Refactor EditCommandTest to set a current operating tutorial in
@BeforeEach via a small helper, and add a simulateEdit helper that
mirrors what EditCommand mutates so expectedModel matches actualModel
under the new tutorial-scoped filter. New test exercises the
no-tutorial-selected failure path.

commitResults.messageTitle: fix: surface invalid-index error for delete command (#227, #228)
commitResults.messageBody:
DeleteCommandParser previously rewrapped all ParseExceptions with
MESSAGE_INVALID_COMMAND_FORMAT, masking the invalid-index message
from ParserUtil.parseIndex when index was 0 or negative.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
